title: psephellus zuvandicus subsp. gegharkunikensis
intro: Psephellus zuvandicus subsp. gegharkunikensis is a rare and endemic subspecies of plants belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae), specifically found in the Gegharkunik Province of Armenia. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Psephellus zuvandicus subsp. gegharkunikensis include its potential to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusions, decoctions, or topical applications to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
